prerequisites: A tyntec account with access to the Business Center. The SMS API is not self-serve for keys: request access via the tyntec contact form to obtain credentials.
An API key copied from the Business Center under API Settings. tyntec issues separate trial and live keys, so use the trial key for testing and switch to the live key for production.
An SMS service configured in the Business Center: log into the Business Center, open the SMS section, and configure a 'Web gateway (REST)' so the REST API can send messages.
During the trial period, the destination number must be whitelisted in the Business Center before messages will be delivered.
A sender ID for the 'from' field: either a phone number in E.164 format or an alphanumeric identifier of up to 11 characters.

authMethods.name: API key (apikey header)
authMethods.summary: The connector's only secret, api_key, maps to tyntec's apikey HTTP header. Every request to the messaging API is authorized by sending the header 'apikey: <your key>'. Obtain the key from the Business Center under API Settings (request access through tyntec if you do not yet have an account).

setupSteps.title: Get tyntec credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the tyntec Business Center. If you do not have access, request it via the tyntec contact form. Open API Settings and copy your API key. Use the trial key while testing and the live key in production.

setupSteps.title: Configure the SMS service
setupSteps.body: In the Business Center, open the SMS section and configure a 'Web gateway (REST)' so the REST messaging API can send SMS. While on a trial key, whitelist the destination phone number you intend to send to.

setupSteps.title: Set the api_key field
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied API key into the connector's api_key field. The connector sends it as the 'apikey' HTTP header on each call to https://api.tyntec.com/messaging/v1/sms, which is how tyntec authenticates the request.

setupSteps.title: Configure the message fields
setupSteps.body: Set 'from' to your sender ID, a phone number in E.164 format or an alphanumeric sender of up to 11 characters, and set 'to' to the destination number in E.164 international format. Optionally set 'message' for the SMS content. These map directly to the from, to, and message fields of tyntec's send-SMS request body.
